attribution_windows.duration: from task claim to task closure, or from request update to next closure

candidate_source_requirements: Persisted text or code artifact in the approved denominator or evidence corpus.
Stable path and SHA-256 hash at measurement time.
Known authority class and claim ceiling.
Consent/privacy label sufficient for the intended ledger visibility.

contribution_event_classes.counterfactual: Replay the production step without the source token, or compare against a matched baseline artifact that had no access to the source token.
contribution_event_classes.definition: A later durable artifact explicitly derives from a source token through citation, path reference, commit reference, or provenance edge.
minimum_evidence: operator or reviewer acceptance if the artifact is public-facing
negative_results: artifact exists but counterfactual delta is zero or unfavorable

contribution_event_classes.counterfactual: Compare with the documented pre-decision state, or run a blinded proposal generation pass without the source token and score the chosen difference.
contribution_event_classes.definition: An operator-approved decision record cites a source token and changes a choice, priority, acceptance, rejection, or scope boundary.
negative_results: operator motive inferred without a decision record
decision only temporally follows the source token

contribution_event_classes.counterfactual: Run the same gate with the source token or receipt removed, masked, or replaced by the previous baseline receipt.
contribution_event_classes.definition: A prior source token enables a deterministic gate, validator, or claim ceiling to pass or fail correctly on a later artifact.
eligible_artifacts: CI or local deterministic gate output
negative_results: gate is nondeterministic or has hidden dependencies
gate pass relies on unsupported claim language

contribution_event_classes.counterfactual: Compare against the pre-revision copy and the gate result that would have occurred without the source token's bound or citation.
contribution_event_classes.definition: A source token causes public copy to become more accurate, bounded, or source-reconciled without upgrading unsupported Token Capital claims.
negative_results: copy passes only because denied claims were removed manually without provenance

contribution_event_classes.counterfactual: Compare against the previous claim state and require the revision rationale to identify why the source token changed the state.
contribution_event_classes.definition: A source token changes the status of a theory claim by strengthening, weakening, bounding, or falsifying it in a durable research artifact.
negative_results: claim state changes without a cited evidence path
revision imports Shapley-value or appreciation math without a measured utility
revision conflates Carrier Dynamics or provenance with economic value

current_claim_ceiling.allowed_summary: The project may say it has a design for measuring downstream contribution and a candidate artifact stream.
current_claim_ceiling.denied_summary: The project may not say downstream contribution, token appreciation, economic value, or compounding value has been demonstrated.

eligible_downstream_artifacts: vault mirrors with source-controlled canonical counterparts
public copy sources that pass the public-surface gate

evidence_artifacts.role: shows existing downstream field is answer-metric ablation only
evidence_artifacts.title: RAG answer faithfulness and answer-metric ablation receipt

evidence_artifacts.role: separates denominator, indexing, retrieval, answer context, and downstream use
evidence_artifacts.title: Token Capital corpus utilization denominator

evidence_artifacts.role: denies downstream contribution and economic claim upgrades
evidence_artifacts.title: Token Capital claim re-gate v2

evidence_artifacts.role: keeps provenance and consent synergies separate from Token Capital proof
evidence_artifacts.title: Carrier Dynamics formalization track

excluded_signals: answer-context exposure without a later artifact
model-drafted text with no operator or reviewer acceptance
temporal proximity without a derivation edge
engagement, attention, or aesthetic preference without a measured artifact outcome
private or consent-sensitive content that cannot be logged with approved labels

acceptance: Ledger schema implements the measurement record fields and fail-closed defaults.
Validator rejects records without artifact hashes, attribution edge, privacy label, counterfactual method, or negative-result status.
Read-only ingest covers source receipts, closed cc-tasks, and public-surface gate receipts without scraping private content or inferring operator motive.
A fixture run records at least one positive, one negative, and one privacy-blocked example using synthetic or existing public-safe fixtures.
The ledger receipt states that no Token Capital claim upgrade is allowed until a future public claim gate explicitly permits it.
first_followup_task.title: Implement downstream contribution ledger v0 instrumentation
first_followup_task.wsjf_formula: BV 8, TC 6, RR/OE 8, size 1.4 -> WSJF 15.7, rounded to 16.0

instrumentable_event_stream.description: A first ledger can be built from existing source-controlled receipts, PR metadata, closed cc-task closure evidence, public-surface gate outputs, and explicit path/hash references. This is an artifact stream, not operator activity surveillance.
inputs: GitHub PR numbers and merge commits recorded in task closure evidence
limits: Cannot infer downstream contribution from retrieval logs alone.
Cannot infer operator motive without an explicit operator-visible decision record.
Cannot upgrade public claims until a future ledger run and public gate permit it.

methodology_references.role: Use entities, activities, agents, derivations, attribution, and primary source relations as the minimum provenance vocabulary.
methodology_references.title: W3C PROV-DM

methodology_references.role: Treat contribution as a potential-outcome question: compare observed artifact outcome with a specified no-source-token counterfactual.
methodology_references.title: Rubin 1974 causal effects of treatments

methodology_references.role: Keep causal assumptions explicit instead of inferring contribution from association, retrieval, or temporal proximity.
methodology_references.title: Pearl 2009 causal inference overview

methodology_references.role: Use predeclared event and estimation windows for any event-study-style diagnostic; do not treat a window alone as causal proof.
methodology_references.title: MacKinlay 1997 event studies in economics and finance

methodology_references.role: Reserve influence-style attribution for future model-training influence questions; current RAG evidence should use replay/ablation first.
methodology_references.title: Koh and Liang 2017 influence functions

metric_layers.question: Can an approved source be indexed, retrieved, and placed in answer context?
Does a generated or extractive answer faithfully support required claims?
Did a source token change a later durable artifact or operator decision?

negative_result_statuses.meaning: The source token was retrieved or read, but no durable later artifact exists.
The later artifact has no explicit citation, derivation, or decision record.
Leave-one-out or matched baseline comparison shows no useful delta.
The source token worsened claim accuracy, gate behavior, or artifact quality.
The event cannot be logged without violating consent or disclosure limits.
The downstream artifact relied on an answer that failed support or faithfulness checks.

privacy_operator_agency_constraints: No hidden operator surveillance: count explicit artifact and decision records, not inferred motive.
No non-operator person data unless consent labels and redaction policy allow the record.
Log hashes, paths, claim classes, and short bounded excerpts only when public-safe.
Operator veto or override can block event logging without being treated as a negative motive.
Consent revocation or missing labels produces a fail-closed privacy_or_consent_blocked event.
